title: Launch Vehicle Design
shortSummary: Integrating engines, structures, avionics, and payload into a complete launch vehicle — the systems engineering challenge of making it all work together.
whyItMatters: A rocket is not just an engine bolted to a fuel tank. Launch vehicle design is the ultimate systems engineering challenge — balancing propulsion performance, structural mass, avionics, aerodynamics, and payload requirements into a vehicle that actually reaches orbit. This is where all the fundamentals come together.
coreConcepts: Launch pad interface and ground systems
